github._comment: GitHub-maintained remote MCP server. Prefer OAuth/Auth CodeLens in your IDE when available instead of storing PATs in config.

filesystem-readonly-example._comment_path: Set COPILOT_FILESYSTEM_PATH to a specific project directory only. NEVER set to /, your home directory, or a secrets directory.

_alternative_pat_config._comment: Inactive example only. If OAuth is unavailable, replace the active github server with this shape. GITHUB_MCP_TOKEN must be fine-grained, revocable, and scoped to the exact repos/actions needed.

_security_notes: NEVER commit mcp.json with real tokens or personal machine paths.
Commit this example template only; keep real IDE/user config local.
Prefer OAuth for the GitHub MCP server when supported by your IDE.
If using a PAT, use a fine-grained token with the minimum repo and action permissions.
Start with read-only toolsets. Enable write tools only with a human approval gate.
Scope filesystem access to specific project directories only.
MCP is tool access, not just context. Treat every enabled tool like a permission grant.
For Business/Enterprise, verify the MCP servers in Copilot policy and registry/allowlist requirements.
_setup_instructions: 1. Copy this file to the MCP config location required by your IDE or Copilot CLI surface.
2. Prefer the GitHub remote MCP server with OAuth/Auth CodeLens when available.
3. If using the PAT example, export GITHUB_MCP_TOKEN with a fine-grained token.
4. If using filesystem access, export COPILOT_FILESYSTEM_PATH to one safe project path.
5. Reload the IDE/CLI session and verify the GitHub server appears in the MCP server list.
6. Open the tool picker and disable write-capable tools unless the workflow explicitly requires them.
